body.title: Igor as clan-template instance; memory ownership by device
body.result: C-clan-template concept node; igor.yaml template_id+memory_domain; granny.yaml+vetinari.yaml memory_domain; rack namespace documented
body.description:
Two related architectural moves: (1) Igor becomes an instance of a clan template — the template carries clan-shared memories and patterns, individual Igor instances layer their own memories on top. A network of agents (per Scott McGregor's vision) share patterns via the clan layer. (2) Memory ownership is sharded by domain: Granny owns all build-related memories, Vetinari owns all task-management and project-status memories. Igor owns cognition/personal memories. This prevents memory sprawl and makes ownership explicit.

Separate from this: the rack gets its own segment of the bus/IMAP infrastructure — no longer co-opting TheIgors' shared namespace. Public feed layer on the bus (MurderBot-style 'feeds' on top of MCP/Hidden IMAP) with no intelligence in the bus itself.

**Affected files:** TBD — discovery step; palace.concepts.* (clan concept node); devices/igor/config or profile (template shape); skeleton/registry.py (memory ownership field?)

**Design rules:** palace-first for concept definitions; bus+shim model for rack segment; no-sqlite

**Scope boundary:** IN — clan template concept + palace node; Igor instance shape; memory ownership declaration (Granny, Vetinari, Igor domains); rack segment namespace. OUT — full multi-Igor deployment; security layer on bus; clan pattern replication protocol

**Completion criteria:** (1) palace concept node C-clan-template exists; (2) Igor's profile declares template_id; (3) Granny and Vetinari profiles declare memory_domain; (4) rack has its own IMAP namespace segment distinct from TheIgors
